title: Low latency combined clock data recovery logic network and charge pump circuit
abstract: Methods and systems are described for obtaining a sequence of data decisions and an error signal generated by one or more samplers operating on a received input signal according to a sampling clock, applying the sequence of data decisions and the error signal to each logic branch of a set of logic branches, and responsively selecting a logic branch from the set of logic branches, the logic branch selected responsive to (i) a detection of a transitional data pattern in the sequence of data decisions and (ii) the error signal, the selected logic branch generating an output current, and providing the output current to a local oscillator controller, the output current sourcing and sinking current to a capacitor through a resistive element to adjust an input voltage of a proportional control circuit relative to a voltage on the capacitor connected to the resistive element.
claims: Claims CLAIMS We claim: 1. A method comprising: obtaining a sequence of data decisions from a history buffer and an error signal, the sequence of data decisions and the error signal generated by one or more samplers operating on a received input signal according to a sampling clock; applying the sequence of data decisions and the error signal to each logic branch of a set of logic branches, and responsively selecting a logic branch from the set of logic branches, the logic branch selected responsive to (i) a detection of a transitional data pattern in the sequence of data decisions and (ii) the error signal, the selected logic branch generating an output current; and providing the output current to a local oscillator controller to adjust an input voltage of a proportional control circuit, the output current sourcing and sinking current to a capacitor through a resistive element to adjust the input voltage of the proportional control circuit relative to a voltage on the capacitor connected to the resistive element. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising adjusting an input voltage of an integral control circuit. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein adjusting the input voltage of the integral control circuit comprises sourcing and sinking a portion of the output current to a second capacitor via a second resistive element. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the input voltage of the integral control circuit is adjusted responsive to a change in the voltage on the capacitor connected to the resistive element. 5. The method of claim 2, wherein adjusting the input voltage of the integral control circuit comprises generating an analog voltage using a digital-to-analog converter (DAC). 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the analog voltage is generated from accumulating error signals in a multi-bit register. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the sequence of data decisions and the error signal are applied to each logic branch via respective sets of series-connected transistors in the logic branch. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the sequence of data decisions and error signal are obtained from samplers applying speculative DFE correction values to the received input signal. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein the received input signal corresponds to an output of a multi-input comparator. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the received input signal corresponds to one data phase of a plurality of data phases. 11. An apparatus comprising: one or more samplers configured to operate on a received input signal according to a sampling clock, the one or more samplers further configured to generate a sequence of data decisions obtained from a history buffer and an error signal; a set of logic branches, each logic branch receiving the sequence of data decisions and the error signal, each logic branch from the set of logic branches configured to be selected in accordance with the sequence of data decisions and the error signal responsive to (i) a detection of a transitional data pattern in the sequence of data decisions and (ii) the error signal, the selected logic branch configured to generate an output current; and a local oscillator controller configured to receive the output current and to adjust an input voltage of a proportional control circuit, the output current sourcing and sinking current to a capacitor through a resistive element to adjust the input voltage of the proportional control circuit relative to a voltage on the capacitor connected to the resistive element. 12. The apparatus of claim 11, further comprising an integral control circuit, and wherein the local oscillator controller is configured to adjust an input voltage to the integral control circuit. 13. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the local oscillator controller is configured to adjust the input voltage of the integral control circuit by sourcing and sinking a portion of the output current to a second capacitor via a second resistive element. 14. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the input voltage of the integral control circuit is adjusted responsive to a change in the voltage on the capacitor connected to the resistive element. 15. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the local oscillator controller comprises a digital-to- analog converter (DAC) configured to adjust the input voltage of the integral control circuit 16. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the DAC comprises a multi-bit register configured to accumulate error signals to adjust the input voltage of the integral control circuit. 17. The apparatus of claim 11, wherein each logic branch comprises a respective set of series-connected transistors configured to receive the sequence of data decisions and the error signal. 18. The apparatus of claim 11, wherein the one or more sampler are configured to apply speculative DFE correction values to the received input signal. 19. The apparatus of claim 11, further comprising a multi-input comparator configured to generate the received input signal. 20. The apparatus of claim 11, wherein the received input signal corresponds to one data phase of a plurality of data phases.
